Reports state Michael had two handgun magazines and a baton on him with bullet wounds to his right shoulder blade and the top left of his head.
Melody Perry was flown back to Fairbanks for questioning aboard Helo 2, which landed on the highway for the nearly 40-minute transport to Fairbanks.
Michael had a lengthy criminal history, including multiple sexual assault charges and a violent assault conviction for pouring acid on his wife's face.
When officers contacted his wife over 2,500 miles away in Texas, she said they hadn't spoken in about six months.
According to the police report, she had previously changed her name to Michael Grimes, matching her now deceased husband's name, but was in the process of changing it back.
Reports state suspects Michael and Melody were in an intimate relationship.
She told officers he ordered her to get on the floor of the vehicle just before he initially fired.

In the days that followed, a search of the vehicle found multiple guns, 2.678 kilos or 5.9 pounds of methamphetamines, 257.5 grams or 9 ounces of fentanyl powder, 111 grams or 3.92 ounces and about 1,000 blue fentanyl pills, drug paraphernalia indicating distribution, hundreds of rounds of mixed caliber ammunition, and over $6,300 in cash.
The truck's damage showed bullet fragments on the tailgate with shots traveling from the back window to the windshield and fragments in the driver's side headrest.
Four officers fired their weapons during the incident, prompting a deadly force investigation.
No officers were harmed in this video, despite multiple cruisers taking gunfire.
Though officers and news sources initially reported Michael was shot and killed by police, his autopsy confirmed he died from his own handgun.
Forensic bullet trajectory matched his hat and the truck's roof.
According to the police report, Melody claimed his final words were to tell police whatever they wanted to know.
Police notified Michael's daughter as next of kin.
The report notes a text she sent to officers, I feel really bad for the troopers.
I don't want them to lose sleep at night.
Our family speculated for quite some time that when his actions caught up to him, that death by cop would be a very possible course of action.
Breaks my heart that he was too cowardly to face the consequences of his actions and chose to put that burden on law enforcement.
I want them to know this was a choice he made and we are thinking of them and their families during this time.
He was on borrowed time and it was inevitable.
In this case, Melody was charged with one count of felon in possession of a weapon.
Beyond her actions in the video, officers also obtained footage of her and Michael openly carrying and buying firearm accessories at Alaska Ammo.
Footage that contributed to the approved search warrant and felony stop.
Investigators found the weapons they were armed with were bought by a third party at Sportsman's Warehouse by an acquaintance who illegally purchased them under their own name.
A search of Michael's home uncovered the receipt with a handwritten note reading, Happy Anniversary Mike and Mel, and hers purple, his white, matching the description of the handguns found on scene.
Surveillance also showed the purchase with the third party presumably using a card in their own name.
The purchaser was indicted on one count of making a false statement during a firearm purchase.
The report also suggests others may have bought weapons for the pair, though this remains unconfirmed.
According to the Alaska Department of Corrections, Melody was sentenced for one count of felon in possession of a weapon and was incarcerated.
Her release is scheduled to coincide with the publishing of this video.
